title: Data Continuation to Transition Table Proof Surface
canonical_claim: Admissibility is not local boundary compliance. Admissibility is recoverable convergence across coherent, evolving, coupled boundary fields.

stages.name: Same-data role dependence
stages.summary: The same datum can receive ALLOW, ALLOW_WITH_SIGNOFF, or FAIL_CLOSED depending on the consequence-bearing role and transition.

stages.name: Compound and temporal admissibility
stages.proof_claim: local allow + local allow does not imply composite allow
stages.summary: Compound consequence mass, commit-time state drift, inference-window collapse, recoverability floor failure, and replay-as-reversal each produce fail-closed conditions.

stages.name: Transition classes as admissibility contracts
stages.proof_claim: a transition type is not only a label; it is an admissibility contract
stages.summary: Theorem-backed transitions are represented as transition classes with explicit consequence mass, legitimacy capacity, recoverability, inference-window, commit-time, replay, boundary, coupling, and outcome constraints.

stages.name: System-coherent boundary transition classes
stages.proof_claim: boundary transitions must remain recoverably convergent and system-coherent
stages.summary: Next proof layer should add transition classes for system-coherence failure, purpose-convergence failure, boundary reset, boundary evolution, and multi-body coupling stress cases.
